title: CPE C2 Proficiency Reading & Use of English - Practice Test 3 (Book 3)
source: CPE Practice Tests 3, Virginia Evans, Express Publishing

sections.title: Part 1: Multiple-Choice Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 1-8, read the text below and decide which answer (A, B, C or D) best fits each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
Part 1: Cloze Texts

Farming Made Easy

Only a few years ago, crop farmers were fighting the cruel hand of fate with their backs firmly against the wall. Overseas production had driven down demand, resulting in (1) ______ bottom prices. As if that wasn't bad enough, a host of pesticide-resistant insects were eating (2) ______ way through the crops, and growers were almost powerless to stop them. Today, even if things aren't as (3) ______ as growers would like them to be, pest control isn't nearly the headache it was a decade ago. With the (4) ______ of a whole new family of insecticides, the job is likely to get even easier. We've got the best tools to control insects that we have ever had in the (5) ______ history of synthetic chemicals. But for now the issue is money. The new chemistry is very expensive and, unless many farmers haven't had an incentive to use it on a (6) ______ scale, production is bound to drop.

The Old House

The old house had sat (7) ______ for many years, its thatched roof slowly falling into disrepair. (8) ______ by tall trees, it was situated near a row of new semi-detached residences. Few noticed it sitting there all alone and forlorn.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: Gap (1):
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'rock bottom prices' là cụm từ cố định (collocation) có nghĩa là 'giá chạm sàn/giá thấp nhất có thể'. Phù hợp với ngữ cảnh cạnh tranh nước ngoài đè bẹp giá nông sản. Bẫy: 'cheap' (rẻ) không đi với 'bottom', 'stone' thường đi với 'deaf' hoặc 'cold', 'every' không tạo ý nghĩa phù hợp. Chuyên đề: Collocations.
questions.answerLocation: resulting in rock bottom prices.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (2):
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'eating their way through' là cấu trúc diễn đạt mang tính thành ngữ nghĩa là 'ăn tàn phá hết/ăn xuyên qua cái gì' (của loài sâu hại). Sử dụng tính từ sở hữu 'their' thay thế cho 'pesticide-resistant insects'. Chuyên đề: Idiomatic expressions.
questions.answerLocation: insects were eating their way through the crops
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (3):
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. 'rosy' (hồng hào/thuận lợi) dùng trong cấu trúc so sánh 'as rosy as' nghĩa là 'thuận lợi/tốt đẹp như mong đợi'. Bẫy: 'happy' không dùng cho sự vật theo cách này, 'flowery' là nhiều hoa/văn hoa, 'strong' là mạnh mẽ. Chuyên đề: Từ vựng — Adjectives.
questions.answerLocation: Today, even if things aren't as rosy as growers would like
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (4):
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'with the development of...' = với sự phát triển/sự ra đời của một dòng thuốc trừ sâu mới. Bẫy: 'research' (nghiên cứu) cần giới từ 'into', 'freedom' (tự do) và 'discharge' (sự giải phóng/thải ra) không hợp ngữ cảnh. Chuyên đề: Từ vựng — Nouns.
questions.answerLocation: With the development of a whole new family of insecticides
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (5):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'in the entire history of...' = trong toàn bộ lịch sử của... Đây là collocation cố định nhấn mạnh tính bao quát lịch sử. Bẫy: 'full' và 'complete' ít tự nhiên hơn khi đi với 'history' trong cấu trúc nhấn mạnh này, 'perfect' nghĩa là hoàn hảo. Chuyên đề: Collocations.
questions.answerLocation: we have ever had in the entire history of synthetic chemicals.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (6):
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. 'on a large scale' là cụm giới từ cố định (collocation) cực kỳ phổ biến nghĩa là 'ở quy mô lớn'. Bẫy: các tính từ khác như 'great', 'long', 'total' không tạo thành cụm từ chỉ quy mô. Chuyên đề: Collocations.
questions.answerLocation: incentive to use it on a large scale
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (7):
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. 'sat derelict' = bị bỏ hoang, vô chủ. Từ 'derelict' mô tả trạng thái một tòa nhà bị bỏ hoang lâu ngày dẫn đến xuống cấp. Bẫy: 'annihilated' (bị tiêu diệt hoàn toàn), 'destroyed' (bị phá hủy), 'decadent' (suy đồi/thối nát). Chuyên đề: Từ vựng — Tính từ mô tả nhà cửa.
questions.answerLocation: The old house had sat derelict for many years
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (8):
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. 'Flanked by tall trees' = được bao quanh/ở bên cạnh bởi những hàng cây cao lớn. Động từ 'flank' nghĩa là sườn bên, ở bên cạnh. Bẫy: 'Lined' thường dùng cho đường đi (lined with trees), 'Edged' là viền quanh, 'Opposed' là phản đối/đối diện. Chuyên đề: Từ vựng — Phân biệt phân từ.
questions.answerLocation: Flanked by tall trees, it was situated near a row
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 1, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 2: Open Cloze
sections.instructions: For questions 9-16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap.
sections.passageHtml:
Radio

The advent of television could have meant the end of the line for radio broadcasting, but over half a century later it is still going (9) ______. Every morning, listeners across the country tune (10) ______ to their favourite station as they wake up, drive to work or start going (11) ______ their daily chores. As social beings, we naturally (12) ______ out the company of others so, in situations where we are alone, the radio becomes the perfect surrogate companion.

As a result, in (13) ______ to watching television, (14) ______ to the radio tends to be more of a solitary pastime. So, what kind of entertainment is on (15) ______? Well, there is (16) ______ a wide range of broadcasts available that radio caters to almost all tastes: music, talk shows, documentaries, comedy — you name it, radio’s got it.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct word in each gap.

questions.instruction: Gap (9): but over half a century later it is still going ______.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: STRONG. Thành ngữ cố định 'still going strong' nghĩa là 'vẫn đang hoạt động mạnh mẽ/vẫn rất thịnh hành'. Chuyên đề: Idiomatic expressions.
questions.answerLocation: over half a century later it is still going strong.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (10): listeners across the country tune ______ to their favourite...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IN. Phrasal verb 'tune in (to something)' nghĩa là 'bật đài/đón xem một chương trình phát thanh hoặc truyền hình'. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: listeners across the country tune in to their favourite station
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (11): start going ______ their daily chores.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: ABOUT. Phrasal verb 'go about something' nghĩa là 'bắt đầu thực hiện/làm một công việc thường nhật'. 'Go about daily chores' = bắt tay làm công việc nhà hàng ngày. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: start going about their daily chores.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (12): we naturally ______ out the company of others
questions.explanation: Đáp án: SEEK. Phrasal verb 'seek out' nghĩa là 'tìm kiếm/săn đón'. Con người là sinh vật xã hội nên tự nhiên sẽ luôn tìm kiếm sự đồng hành của người khác. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: we naturally seek out the company of others
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (13): As a result, in ______ to watching television...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: CONTRAST. Cụm từ liên kết 'in contrast to something' = trái ngược với cái gì. Nhằm đối lập hoạt động xem TV (thường mang tính tập thể) với nghe đài (mang tính đơn độc). Chuyên đề: Fixed expressions.
questions.answerLocation: As a result, in contrast to watching television
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (14): ______ to the radio tends to be...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: LISTENING. Danh động từ 'listening' làm chủ ngữ chính cho mệnh đề: 'listening to the radio' (việc nghe đài phát thanh). Đi kèm giới từ 'to'. Chuyên đề: Gerunds. Meo/Trap: xac dinh loai gap truoc khi chon: word form, collocation, dependent preposition, clause pattern hoac idiom. Kiem tra tu ngay truoc/sau gap, so it/so nhieu, tense/aspect va register. Bay thuong gap la dap an dung nghia nhung sai cau truc hoac sai collocation.
questions.answerLocation: listening to the radio tends to be more of a solitary pastime.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (15): what kind of entertainment is on ______?
questions.explanation: Đáp án: OFFER. Cụm giới từ cố định 'on offer' nghĩa là 'sẵn có/được cung cấp'. 'What is on offer' = những thứ gì đang sẵn có. Chuyên đề: Collocations.
questions.answerLocation: So, what kind of entertainment is on offer?
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Gap (16): there is ______ a wide range of broadcasts available that...
questions.explanation: Đáp án: SUCH. Cấu trúc so sánh kết quả nhấn mạnh 'such + Noun Phrase + that...': 'there is such a wide range... that...' (có một sự đa dạng lớn các chương trình phát sóng đến nỗi mà...). Chuyên đề: Grammar — intensifiers.
questions.answerLocation: there is such a wide range of broadcasts available that
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 2, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 3: Word Formation
sections.instructions: For questions 17-24, read the text below. Use the word given in capitals at the end of some of the lines to form a word that fits in the space in the same line.
sections.passageHtml:
Transatlantic Travel

At one time, for the majority of people needing to make the journey, the only way to travel from Europe to America was by ship, but the days of a leisurely 5-day (17) ______ (ATLANTIC) crossing in a large liner have long gone. Today, everything is about speed, and long-distance travel has become almost (18) ______ (EXCLUSIVE) the business of the airlines.

Although air travel is (19) ______ (ADMIT) fast, passengers are still subject to (20) ______ (EXPECT) hold-ups which seem to be inherent in any form of travel, but people’s reactions to such delays seem to be far more (21) ______ (PREDICT) than in the days of the great liners. Then, passengers might have shrugged their shoulders (22) ______ (RESIGN) and relaxed with a book in their cabins, knowing that little could be done about the situation, but today’s traveller is more likely to make his or her (23) ______ (SATISFY) known forcibly to any official who is (24) ______ (FORTUNE) enough to appear on the horizon.

questionGroups.instruction: Write the correct transformed word in each gap.

questions.instruction: 17. ATLANTIC ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: TRANSATLANTIC. Danh từ riêng 'ATLANTIC' được thêm tiền tố 'trans-' tạo thành tính từ 'transatlantic' nghĩa là 'xuyên Đại Tây Dương'. Cụm 'transatlantic crossing' = chuyến vượt Đại Tây Dương. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Prefixes.
questions.answerLocation: leisurely 5-day transatlantic crossing
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 18. EXCLUDE ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: EXCLUSIVELY. Động từ 'EXCLUDE' (loại trừ) được chuyển thành trạng từ 'exclusively' nghĩa là 'duy nhất/độc quyền'. Bổ nghĩa cho cụm từ chỉ sở hữu/kinh doanh phía sau. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adverbs.
questions.answerLocation: has become almost exclusively the business of the airlines.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 19. ADMIT ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: ADMITTEDLY. Động từ 'ADMIT' (thừa nhận) chuyển đổi thành trạng từ nhận định 'admittedly' đứng đầu cụm tính từ nghĩa là 'phải thừa nhận là/thật lòng mà nói'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adverbs.
questions.answerLocation: Although air travel is admittedly fast
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 20. EXPECT ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: UNEXPECTED. Động từ 'EXPECT' (trông đợi) được biến đổi thành tính từ mang tiền tố phủ định 'unexpected' nghĩa là 'bất ngờ/không mong đợi/không lường trước được' để bổ nghĩa cho danh từ 'hold-ups' (trì hoãn). Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adjectives.
questions.answerLocation: subject to unexpected hold-ups
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 21. PREDICT ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: UNPREDICTABLE. Động từ 'PREDICT' (dự đoán) được chuyển thành tính từ mang ý nghĩa phủ định 'unpredictable' (khó lường/khó dự đoán) bổ nghĩa cho từ chỉ phản ứng của hành khách. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adjectives.
questions.answerLocation: seem to be far more unpredictable than in the days
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 22. RESIGN ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: RESIGNEDLY. Động từ 'RESIGN' (cam chịu/từ chức) chuyển hóa thành trạng từ 'resignedly' nghĩa là 'một cách cam chịu/đành chấp nhận số phận'. Bổ nghĩa cho hành động nhún vai 'shrugged their shoulders'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Adverbs.
questions.answerLocation: shrugged their shoulders resignedly and relaxed
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 23. SATISFY ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: DISSATISFACTION. Động từ 'SATISFY' (làm thỏa mãn) cần được chuyển thành danh từ mang tiền tố phủ định 'dissatisfaction' (sự bất mãn/không hài lòng), đứng sau tính từ sở hữu 'his or her'. Hành khách thời nay dễ dàng biểu lộ sự bất mãn của mình. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Negative nouns.
questions.answerLocation: make his or her dissatisfaction known forcibly
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 24. FORTUNE ->
questions.explanation: Đáp án: UNFORTUNATE. Danh từ 'FORTUNE' (sự may mắn/gia sản) biến đổi thành tính từ mang ý phủ định 'unfortunate' (không may/kém may mắn) làm bổ ngữ đứng trước 'enough to appear...'. Chuyên đề: Word formation — Negative prefixes.
questions.answerLocation: any official who is unfortunate enough to appear
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 3, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 4: Key Word Transformation
sections.instructions: For questions 25-30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between three and eight words, including the word given.

questionGroups.instruction: Complete the second sentence using the key word in bold.

questions.instruction: Mark was very surprised to see that his watch had been returned. (surprise) -> Much ____________________ someone had returned his watch.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: TO HIS SURPRISE, SOMEONE. Sử dụng cụm từ diễn đạt trạng thái cảm xúc cố định 'Much to one's surprise' (Trước sự vô cùng ngạc nhiên của ai). Kết hợp đại từ bất định 'someone' đóng vai trò chủ ngữ cho vế sau 'had returned his watch'. Chuyên đề: Grammar & Fixed phrases.
questions.answerLocation: to his surprise, someone
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: The only choice he had was to keep quiet. (no) -> He ____________________ keep quiet.
acceptedAnswers: was left with no choice but to
questions.explanation: Đáp án: HAD NO CHOICE BUT TO. Cấu trúc cố định nhấn mạnh sự bắt buộc duy nhất: 'have no choice/alternative but + to-infinitive' (không còn sự lựa chọn nào khác ngoài việc làm gì). Chuyên đề: Idiomatic grammar.
questions.answerLocation: had no choice but to
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Katy hasn’t been feeling well lately. (run) -> Katy has ____________________ lately.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: BEEN FEELING RUN DOWN (hoặc BEEN RUN DOWN). Sử dụng phrasal adjective 'run down' để mô tả tình trạng sức khỏe suy nhược, mệt mỏi, kiệt sức. Kết hợp thì hiện tại hoàn thành tiếp diễn 'has been feeling run down' để nhấn mạnh tính liên tục gần đây. Chuyên đề: Phrasal verbs & Tenses.
questions.answerLocation: been feeling run down
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: I admire her very much. (full) -> I am ____________________ her.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: FULL OF ADMIRATION FOR. Chuyển đổi động từ 'admire' (ngưỡng mộ) thành cụm tính từ cố định đi kèm danh từ: 'be full of admiration for somebody' (đầy lòng ngưỡng mộ đối với ai). Chuyên đề: Collocations & Prepositions.
questions.answerLocation: full of admiration for
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: Nothing is stopping you from entering the competition. (why) -> There’s no ____________________ enter the competition.
questions.explanation: Đáp án: REASON WHY YOU SHOULDN'T. Sử dụng mệnh đề quan hệ trạng từ chỉ lý do 'reason why' kết hợp thể phủ định phủ quyết 'should not/shouldn't' để diễn đạt ý kiến khuyên nhủ nhập cuộc: 'Không có lý do gì bạn lại không tham gia cả'. Chuyên đề: Clause synthesis & Modals.
questions.answerLocation: reason why you shouldn't
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: There’s no point in trying to phone her as she’s away for the weekend. (use) -> She’s away for the weekend so ____________________ phone her.
acceptedAnswers: it is no use trying to
there is no use trying to
questions.correctAnswer: it is no use trying to
questions.explanation: Đáp án: IT IS NO USE TRYING TO. Sử dụng cấu trúc thành ngữ giả chủ ngữ 'It is no use + V-ing' (Vô ích khi làm việc gì), tương đương với câu gốc 'There is no point in + V-ing'. Kết hợp động từ 'try to' nguyên mẫu ở vế sau. Chuyên đề: Gerund structures.
questions.answerLocation: it is no use trying to
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 4, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.passageHtml:
Key Word Transformation

25. Mark was very surprised to see that his watch had been returned. (surprise)

Much ____________________ someone had returned his watch.

26. The only choice he had was to keep quiet. (no)

He ____________________ keep quiet.

27. Katy hasn’t been feeling well lately. (run)

Katy has ____________________ lately.

28. I admire her very much. (full)

I am ____________________ her.

29. Nothing is stopping you from entering the competition. (why)

There’s no ____________________ enter the competition.

30. There’s no point in trying to phone her as she’s away for the weekend. (use)

She’s away for the weekend so ____________________ phone her.

sections.title: Part 5: Multiple Choice
sections.instructions: You are going to read an extract from a novel. For questions 31-36, choose the answer (A, B, C or D) which you think fits best according to the text.
sections.passageHtml:
A Friend in Need

I really had no inkling of what was to ensue. We had always been very close of course; that went without saying. Being kept in the dark, therefore, about a matter of such consequence came not so much as a surprise but rather as a source of deep shock. It grieved me to have been hoodwinked, as it were, and at first my refusal to accept the situation clouded my better judgement. So I found myself in the uncomfortable position of being left in the cold and the coveted role of confidante denied me.

Our first encounter dates back a decade. I faintly recall a slender figure brushing past me on the first floor of a rather well-known department store, and as my glance was drawn to follow, my thoughts were interrupted, or perhaps even read: "Makes you sick, doesn’t it. I bet she only drinks water and feeds on morsels of fruit." Instinctively I turned to search for the owner of the voice. Directly behind me stood a woman of my age and my build. What my mother, bless her, had always encouraged in me as a healthy appetite had unfortunately turned to tubbiness in my middle years. "Makes you sick, I say." I suppose that I realised then that the speaker had been addressing me. "Fancy a cup of hot chocolate?" I offered shyly.

Our friendship blossomed. We found that we had a lot in common, and grew more inseparable as the months and years went by. At times our appearance drew the unwanted attention of others: awkward glances, sidelong looks, suppressed giggles. This was not unfamiliar either to me or to Geena I believe, but now the confidence found in the safety of numbers, albeit just two, drew me out of my shell and life took on a completely new outlook. We must have painted quite a picture the pair of us, two rather frumpish middle-aged ladies window-shopping in Oxford Street, making our way in and out of crowds of youngsters half our age. But we hadn't a care in the world; we were having the time of our lives.

However, neglect of one’s health takes its toll and two years ago I was diagnosed with a slight heart problem. Having been referred to a dietician I was faced with a strict regimen, and slowly but surely created a new lifestyle for myself. No longer did I take the bus just to pop into town, nor did I slump in front of the television of an evening, opting instead for a stroll in the local park. Supermarket shopping was the greatest strain; as I religiously avoided aisles previously worshipped. To my mild surprise, Geena seemed to take little interest in my health problem and it became a subject carefully avoided by the pair of us. I was not concerned however, as we continued spending a lot of time together and the ritual outings to the West End every other Saturday were devotedly adhered to. Only now I would order black coffee and toast instead of the former hot chocolate and Danish.

A more astute person, I am sure, would have picked up on the tell-tale signs but I have never been particularly discerning about other people’s feelings, having led a rather withdrawn life. Geena was in fact my sole companion, my mother having passed away years ago, and to be honest I had come to rely so much on her that I believe I was taking her for granted and consequently neglecting her own needs. In retrospect I realize that I was no longer a good friend and any attempt she may have made to communicate with me fell on deaf ears.

I heard from some that her wedding day was beautiful. I would have gone if it hadn't been for a previous arrangement I had made. She was very understanding of course, well that’s what had attracted me to her in the first place - her long-suffering patience and ability to understand. They say she made a lovely bride; apparently she even lost a bit of weight before the ceremony and looked a dream in her wedding outfit. At least I didn’t miss my doctor’s appointment: He’s rather concerned you see, as lately my blood pressure has gone up and I've put on some pounds again. But I’m sure it’s nothing to worry about. Perhaps I could phone Geena for a chat. That would cheer me up, and it’s four weeks now since she got back from her honeymoon. Well, maybe tomorrow. I don’t want to be a bother...

questionGroups.instruction: For questions 31-36, choose the correct letter, A, B, C or D.

questions.instruction: 31. What do we learn in the first paragraph about the narrator’s attitude towards her friend?
options: A. She was upset by her friend’s news, but did not find it unexpected.
B. She felt her friend could have told her the news in a different manner.
C. She wished she had been entrusted with information at an earlier stage.
D. She deemed her assessment of the situation to be better than her friend's.
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Người kể chuyện đau lòng vì bị giấu giếm ('Being kept in the dark') một việc hệ trọng. Cô cảm thấy tủi thân vì vai trò là người tâm sự thân thiết ('coveted role of confidante') đã bị tước đoạt khỏi cô. Điều này cho thấy cô mong muốn được chia sẻ thông tin sớm hơn. Bẫy: (A) sai vì cô hoàn toàn bị sốc cực độ chứ không phải lường trước, (B) và (D) không được nhắc đến làm trọng tâm thái độ trong đoạn này. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: left in the cold and the coveted role of confidante denied me.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 32. What is the significance of the ‘slender figure’ in the story?
options: A. She makes both the ladies feel unwell.
B. On seeing her, the narrator realises she can read minds.
C. She attracts the ladies’ admiration.
D. The ladies’ reaction to her draws them to each other.
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Hình dáng thon thả ('slender figure') lướt qua làm dấy lên sự đố kỵ chung và phản ứng đồng điệu từ Geena ('Makes you sick, doesn't it'). Phản ứng chung đầy tự ti và đố kỵ này đã kết nối hai người phụ nữ trung niên có thân hình đẫy đà lại với nhau, khởi đầu cho tình bạn bền chặt của họ. Bẫy: (A) sai vì họ chỉ đố kỵ chứ không ốm thật, (B) sai vì cụm 'thoughts read' chỉ là lối ẩn dụ đồng cảm ý nghĩ, (C) sai vì họ ghét đố kỵ chứ không phải ngưỡng mộ. Chuyên đề: Reading — Narrative significance. Correct option focus: D - The ladies’ reaction to her draws them to each other.. Meo/Trap: dap an dung thuong la paraphrase cua source, con distractor hay trung keyword nhung sai scope, sai tone, sai doi tuong hoac dao nguoc y. Luon doi chieu answerLocation/evidence truoc khi chon.
questions.answerLocation: turned to search for the owner... Directly behind me stood a woman of my age and my build... friendship blossomed.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 33. It is suggested that one of the narrator’s problems was that she
options: A. felt self-conscious about comments from passers-by.
B. had had an over-indulgent mother.
C. didn't enjoy being surrounded by a lot of people.
D. felt too old to go shopping in Oxford Street.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Người viết chia sẻ rằng thói quen ăn uống tốt mà người mẹ khuyến khích hồi nhỏ ('What my mother... had always encouraged in me as a healthy appetite') không may đã biến thành sự béo phì ở tuổi trung niên ('unfortunately turned to tubbiness in my middle years'). Điều này ám chỉ sự nuông chiều thái quá về dinh dưỡng của người mẹ ('over-indulgent mother') chính là một phần nguyên nhân. Bẫy: (A) và (C) sai vì khi có bạn đồng hành họ rất tự tin, (D) sai vì họ cực kỳ thích mua sắm ở Oxford Street. Chuyên đề: Reading — Inference.
questions.answerLocation: What my mother, bless her, had always encouraged in me as a healthy appetite had unfortunately turned to tubbiness
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 34. Why did the narrator change her lifestyle?
options: A. She started to feel less devoted to religion.
B. She was recommended to do so by a specialist.
C. She was suffering from a serious cardiac condition.
D. She felt her friendship was unhealthy for her.
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Tác giả đã chuyển đổi lối sống lành mạnh, chăm đi bộ và ăn kiêng sau khi được bác sĩ giới thiệu đến một chuyên gia dinh dưỡng ('Having been referred to a dietician I was faced with a strict regimen...'). Chuyên gia dinh dưỡng chính là một 'specialist'. Bẫy: (A) 'religiously' ở đây nghĩa là một cách nghiêm ngặt/đều đặn chứ không liên quan tôn giáo, (C) sai vì tình trạng tim của cô chỉ ở mức độ nhẹ ('slight heart problem'), (D) sai vì tình bạn không phải lý do cô thay đổi lối sống. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed understanding.
questions.answerLocation: diagnosed with a slight heart problem. Having been referred to a dietician I was faced with a strict regimen
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 35. After the discovery of the narrator’s health problem, it seems that Geena
options: A. failed to relate her feelings to the narrator.
B. started to dislike the narrator's company.
C. wanted to discuss the narrator’s condition in detail.
D. kept putting off their days out together.
E. was jealous of the narrator's weight loss.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Sau khi phát hiện bệnh tình, Geena tránh thảo luận chủ đề này và tác giả thừa nhận cô quá vô tâm nên bỏ qua mọi dấu hiệu cảm xúc ẩn giấu ('failed to relate her feelings' / 'any attempt she may have made to communicate... fell on deaf ears'). Tác giả vô tình nhận ra mình đã không làm tròn vai trò một người bạn tốt. Bẫy: (B) và (D) sai vì họ vẫn đi chơi West End đều đặn, (C) hoàn toàn ngược lại thực tế. Chuyên đề: Reading — Detailed analysis.
questions.answerLocation: any attempt she may have made to communicate with me fell on deaf ears.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 36. By the end of the passage it appears that
options: A. the two friends have drifted apart.
B. the narrator is very angry about Geena’s marriage.
C. the narrator will contact her friend very soon.
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Ở cuối đoạn, tác giả tự nhủ có thể gọi cho Geena nhưng lại lập tức trì hoãn: 'Well, maybe tomorrow. I don’t want to be a bother...' (Có lẽ là ngày mai. Tôi không muốn làm phiền cô ấy...). Điều này chứng tỏ tình bạn của họ đã nguội lạnh và họ đang dần xa cách nhau ('drifted apart') sau sự kiện đám cưới mà cô không tham dự. Chuyên đề: Reading — Synthesis & Tone.
questions.answerLocation: Well, maybe tomorrow. I don’t want to be a bother...
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 5, CPE Practice Tests 3

sections.title: Part 6: Gapped Text
sections.instructions: You are going to read an article about a fitness club. Seven paragraphs have been removed from the extract. Choose from the paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap (37-43). There is one extra paragraph which you do not need to use.
sections.passageHtml:
Marchton Sport Club

It's a cold, frosty morning in Marchton and all over town men and women of all ages and backgrounds are rising bright and early, eagerly leaving behind their dream-filled slumber. No hitting the snooze button for them; no snuggling under warm blankets begging in muffled drowsiness for "just five more minutes ..." These are people filled with a sense of purpose, spurred on by a sense of loyalty to routine and in turn rewarded with a ready flow of endorphins.

[ Gap 37 ]

Specifically, I'm referring to the ninety members of the new fitness club situated in central Marchton. Not only are these participants given the opportunity to attend a wide variety of fitness classes or follow personal fitness programmes in state-of-the-art weight rooms, but they also have at their disposal two heated swimming pools and four squash courts, from which a choice can be made by even the most amateur of budding fitness hopefuls. So how do you join?

[ Gap 38 ]

However, as beneficial and tempting as all this may sound, it’s really no good if you have little or no staying power. Getting back to the current members, I quite simply posed the following question: how does one manage to get fit and then stay fit? What folk of mettle keep going and persevere throughout snowy January, drizzly April, and lazy August ...?

[ Gap 39 ]

It almost seems unfair, doesn’t it, such a decision first thing in the morning! We may be faced with that early morning state of drowsiness, but this is where the fight for fitness is either won or lost. It requires strength of mind long before you concern yourself with matters of the body. For beware! As you awake, a little voice suggests to you from your pillow that you don’t really have to get up early and go to the gym ... just five more minutes ...

[ Gap 40 ]

The people of the past, on the other hand, looked ahead. It is clear that they wanted their buildings to be there for future generations. This is corroborated by the fact that, in countries where the climate allows it, they planted trees. Think about it: planting a tree, especially one that will some day grow to be very big, is the ultimate in altruistic behaviour. When you plant an oak sapling, you know very well that you will not see the tree that it will become.

[ Gap 41 ]

There is a third element in today’s way of thinking that is particularly relevant to architecture, and that is the aesthetic element. This is a difficult area to deal with because it is extremely subjective. Beauty is, indeed, very often in the eye of the beholder; we all know what we like, and we all like different things. Even allowing for this, however, most people would probably agree that ‘beautiful’ is not the word they would use to describe the majority of modern buildings.

[ Gap 42 ]

Needless to say, however, there are a few instances when you should miss a workout and it’s very important to listen to what your body is telling you. It is also seriously unwise to take up any form of strenuous physical activity without first consulting your GP. Equally, you should provide your personal fitness trainer with details of your medical history so you can be advised accordingly on a suitable fitness programme.

[ Gap 43 ]

Whatever your age or health condition, however, the same message seems to be loud and clear; some form of physical activity is essential if we are to lead productive, fulfilling, stress-free lives.

questionGroups.instruction: Choose from the paragraphs A-H the one which fits each gap (37-43).

questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Đoạn D đặt câu hỏi tiếp nối tự nhiên từ mô tả lối sống năng động ở đầu bài: 'Now everybody knows that it's good to work out... But who are these people who actually partake in the ritual?'. Ý 'normal, everyday people' ở cuối đoạn D dẫn nhập hoàn hảo đến câu đầu đoạn sau: 'Specifically, I'm referring to the ninety members of the new fitness club...'. Chuyên đề: Gapped Text — Discourse markers.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 37 — between everyday workout habits and the specific Marchton club members
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Đoạn đầu kết thúc bằng câu hỏi 'So how do you join?' (Làm thế nào để gia nhập?). Đoạn A phản hồi trực tiếp bằng cách giải thích các gói thành viên của Marchton Sport Club: 'A Marchton Sport Club boasts two types of annual membership...'. Cụm 'It sounds attractive, doesn't it?' ở cuối đoạn A tạo thế đối lập hoàn hảo với từ nối 'However' ở đoạn sau: 'However, as beneficial and tempting as all this may sound...'. Chuyên đề: Gapped Text — Question & Answer.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 38 — between the joining question and the membership details
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án F. Cuối đoạn trước hỏi làm sao để duy trì động lực vượt qua thời tiết khắc nghiệt. Đoạn F giới thiệu lời chia sẻ từ thành viên bền bỉ Sally: 'Says Sally, one such persevering member:... I think the key moment comes as soon as the alarm goes off. This is when the decision is made...'. Quyết định thức dậy đó nối tiếp ngay bằng câu đầu đoạn sau: 'It almost seems unfair, doesn't it, such a decision first thing in the morning!'. Chuyên đề: Gapped Text — Narrative cohesion.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 39 — between the question on staying power and Sally's key morning decision
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Đoạn trước kết thúc bằng lời cám dỗ từ chiếc gối: 'a little voice suggests... just five more minutes...'. Đoạn E đập tan sự lười biếng đó bằng hành động quyết liệt: 'But this is precisely the moment when you need to insist in no uncertain terms that such a suggestion is plainly ridiculous... battle is won in the bedroom'. Ý này liên kết chặt chẽ với phản đề ở đoạn sau: 'The people of the past, on the other hand, looked ahead...' (ngược lại người xưa luôn nhìn xa trông rộng). Chuyên đề: Gapped Text — Contrasting dialogue.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 40 — between pillow temptation and bedroom battle resolution
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án G. Đoạn trước mô tả trồng một cây sồi nhỏ vị tha, nhìn xa cho tương lai. Đoạn G bắt đầu bằng: 'In contrast to this, today's tree-planting activity is largely commercial...' (ngược lại trồng cây thời nay mang tính thương mại ngắn hạn). Đoạn G so sánh sự ngắn hạn này với thói quen xây nhà hiện đại trước khi dẫn nhập đến yếu tố thứ 3 ở đoạn sau: 'There is a third element in today's way of thinking...'. Chuyên đề: Gapped Text — Semantic contrast.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 41 — between altruistic long-term tree planting and short-term commercial planting
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Đoạn trước phê bình sự xấu xí của các tòa nhà hiện đại và đặt câu hỏi 'Maybe we want a challenge'. Đoạn sau tiếp tục thảo luận về tính trị liệu và an toàn của hoạt động thể chất, kết nối trực tiếp với đoạn C về việc đảm bảo an toàn y tế và đội ngũ y tá sẵn sàng tại câu lạc bộ thể thao khi có sự cố. Chuyên đề: Gapped Text — Coherence.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 42 — between aesthetic challenge and medical/safety support in the fitness club
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.explanation: Đáp án H. Đoạn trước phân tích sự ngắt quãng rèn luyện dễ phá vỡ thói quen dẫn đến bỏ cuộc. Đoạn H đưa ra lời khuyên động viên: 'If you do miss one morning, don't berate yourself, just go the next day...'. Cuối đoạn H đặt câu hỏi mang tính khiêu khích về sự lười biếng thời đại và dẫn đến kết luận mạnh mẽ ở đoạn cuối: 'physical activity is essential if we are to lead productive lives'. Chuyên đề: Gapped Text — Resolution & Conclusion.
questions.answerLocation: Gap 43 — between breaking habits warning and final active lifestyle resolution
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 6, CPE Practice Tests 3

paragraphOptions.label: A
paragraphOptions.text: A Marchton Sport Club boasts two types of annual membership that offer a wide range of facilities depending on personal preference, making it the most versatile fitness membership in the southwest. Options are also available for weekly or monthly passes, in addition to student and OAP concessions. There are also many specialised programmes if you want to try out something more specific or trendy like yoga, weight-watchers or ‘parents and babies’. It sounds attractive, doesn’t it?

paragraphOptions.label: B
paragraphOptions.text: The centre is our largest and newest facility, offering everything that a private club might provide including cafeterias, a four-star restaurant and conference rooms. These amenities are well above the standard to which provincial towns have previously been accustomed, and I would recommend taking up membership to anyone interested in the well-being of Marchton.

paragraphOptions.label: C
paragraphOptions.text: The Director reassures us that, should someone feel distressed or unwell during a training session, this is promptly communicated to a club representative, and that there are always qualified nursing staff on hand ready to administer first aid if the need arises.

paragraphOptions.label: D
paragraphOptions.text: Now everybody knows that it’s good to work out .. that we should work out. But who are these people who actually partake in the ritual? How can they have so much energy, so little stress, and why don’t they look like super-models? Well, they are in fact no more than normal, everyday people, to whom some refer as ‘the chosen ones’ or to be more precise ‘the ones who have chosen’ — chosen to live a healthy balanced lifestyle by exercising on a regular basis.

paragraphOptions.label: E
paragraphOptions.text: But this is precisely the moment when you need to insist in no uncertain terms that such a suggestion is plainly ridiculous, and has been so from the day on which you purchased that membership! Believe it or not, this is one battle that is won in the bedroom and if you can just make it out of bed, (or out of the office or out of the house, depending on what time of day you prefer ...) you’re halfway there!

paragraphOptions.label: F
paragraphOptions.text: Says Sally, one such persevering member: 'Well, to help you keep that new habit fresh, you need a frame of mind that should help you to stay on track and discover a lifetime of fitness freedom. I think the key moment comes as soon as the alarm goes off. This is when the decision is made, in those first seconds of early morning when the sub-conscious is grinding its gears and facing the first challenge of the day: getting out of bed. You must be tough with yourself.'

paragraphOptions.label: G
paragraphOptions.text: There’s a reason for that which I’ve already mentioned, and they’re called ‘endorphins.’ They result from a chemical reaction that takes place in your body after your workout and their main purpose is ... to make you feel good! This is a hormone whose sole function is to create a feeling of well-being. In other words, it’s your body’s way of thanking you for taking care of it.

paragraphOptions.label: H
paragraphOptions.text: 'If you do miss one morning, don’t berate yourself, just go the next day,' she says. 'What you need to be telling yourself is this: okay, this is it. I am choosing right now to have an active lifestyle.' So, are we really up to it? We live after all in a world of ease where physical activity is avoided, even shunned. Imagine the TV without the remote, going any distance on foot or even carrying the supermarket shopping all the way home. Is there really anyone who wants to work out that much?

sections.title: Part 7: Multiple Matching
sections.instructions: You are going to read an article about the evolution of language in the age of social media. For questions 44-53, choose from the sections (A-E). The sections may be chosen more than once.
sections.passageHtml:
The Evolution of Language

Section A: The Digital Lexicon

Historically, language has evolved at a glacial pace, shaped by geographical isolation and formal literary canons. Today, however, the digital revolution has initiated a linguistic transformation of unprecedented speed. Emojis, acronyms, and memes have consolidated into a global digital shorthand. Neologisms and rapid semantic shifts occur overnight, driven by algorithmically powered platforms. While critics argue that this represents an intellectual degradation, descriptivists celebrate this phase as a fertile era of linguistic playfulness and democratized creativity. They contend that rather than decaying, language is exhibiting a robust vitality, adapting dynamically to the unique communicative constraints of virtual spaces.

Section B: Cognitive and Psychological Shifts

The impact of social media language extends far beyond mere vocabulary; it is actively reshaping cognitive processing and psychological self-expression. The brevity demanded by platforms like Twitter or TikTok has cultivated a conversational style characterized by extreme conciseness and emotional hyperbole. Scholars warn that this could lead to a gradual truncation of attention spans and a diminished capacity for sustained, complex narrative synthesis. Conversely, proponents argue that this forced brevity encourages intellectual agility and precision, prompting users to distill complex arguments into potent, high-impact statements. The digital medium also democratizes emotional expression, allowing individuals to articulate mental health struggles or seek solidarity through highly structured, recognizable online idioms.

Section C: The Rise of Digital Sociolects

From a sociological perspective, the internet has facilitated the birth of diverse digital sociolects that serve as powerful markers of group identity. Online subcultures, from gaming forums to book communities, develop their own highly specialized vernaculars and inside jokes. These linguistic codes establish clear boundaries, separating the in-group from the out-group, thereby fostering a deep sense of belonging and mutual understanding among geographically dispersed individuals. However, the speed with which these sociolects evolve can also Social fragmentation can follow. Older generations and those outside specific digital spheres often find themselves completely excluded from conversations, highlighting how digital language can simultaneously act as a powerful unifier and a formidable social barrier.

Section D: The Prescriptivist Backlash

The rapid democratization of language has provoked a fierce backlash from linguistic prescriptivists and educators. They voice deep concern over what they perceive as a systemic decay in formal writing standards among younger generations, citing the frequent intrusion of text-speak, grammatical shortcuts, and erratic punctuation in academic work. They argue that the casual, fractured nature of digital communication undermines the capacity for clear, logical, and formal argumentation, which is essential for professional and academic success. However, progressive educators counter that this view is overly alarmist. They advocate for a model of code-switching, teaching students to appreciate the richness of both formal, traditional English and the creative, highly context-dependent language of their online worlds.

Section E: Cross-Cultural Synthesis

Perhaps the most fascinating aspect of digital language is its globalizing power, acting as a catalyst for cross-cultural synthesis and linguistic borrowing. Internet slang, predominantly rooted in English, rapidly crosses international boundaries, infiltrating other languages and reshaping their everyday vocabularies. This has led to the emergence of hybrid internet dialects worldwide, where local grammar is combined with global digital slang. While some cultural preservationists view this as a form of linguistic imperialism that threatens minor languages, others see it as an organic, hybrid evolution. It illustrates how digital communication bridges geographical gaps, fostering a shared global youth culture that transcends national borders while continuously adapting to local contexts.

questionGroups.instruction: For questions 44-53, choose from the sections A-E. The sections may be chosen more than once.

questions.instruction: 44. the view that digital communication bridges geographical gaps
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Section E chỉ rõ: 'It illustrates how digital communication bridges geographical gaps, fostering a shared global youth culture...' (Nó minh họa cách thức truyền thông số làm cầu nối cho các khoảng cách địa lý, nuôi dưỡng một nền văn hóa giới trẻ toàn cầu chung...). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Text matching.
questions.answerLocation: Section E: digital communication bridges geographical gaps, fostering a shared global youth culture
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 45. a warning about the potential truncation of attention spans
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Section B nêu lời cảnh báo: 'Scholars warn that this could lead to a gradual truncation of attention spans and a diminished capacity for sustained, complex narrative synthesis.' (Các học giả cảnh báo điều này có thể dẫn đến sự rút ngắn dần khoảng chú ý và làm giảm khả năng tổng hợp tự sự phức tạp kéo dài). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Paraphrasing.
questions.answerLocation: Section B: Scholars warn that this could lead to a gradual truncation of attention spans
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 46. how online sociolects can simultaneously unify and exclude
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Section C nhấn mạnh tính hai mặt của ngôn ngữ mạng xã hội: 'digital language can simultaneously act as a powerful unifier and a formidable social barrier.' (ngôn ngữ số đồng thời có thể hoạt động như một chất kết dính mạnh mẽ và là một rào cản xã hội ghê gớm, loại trừ những người bên ngoài). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Synthesis.
questions.answerLocation: Section C: simultaneously act as a powerful unifier and a formidable social barrier.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 47. a rejection of the idea that language is experiencing intellectual degradation
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Section A bác bỏ quan điểm suy đồi ngôn ngữ: 'While critics argue that this represents an intellectual degradation... descriptivists celebrate... rather than decaying, language is exhibiting a robust vitality...' (Trong khi các nhà phê bình cho rằng điều này đại diện cho sự suy thoái trí tuệ... những nhà miêu tả lại tôn vinh... thay vì phân rã, ngôn ngữ đang thể hiện một sức sống mạnh mẽ...). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Synonymous matching.
questions.answerLocation: Section A: Rather than decaying, language is exhibiting a robust vitality
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 48. the promotion of code-switching as a way to appreciate different styles of language
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Section D đề cập giải pháp giáo dục tiến bộ: 'They advocate for a model of code-switching, teaching students to appreciate the richness of both formal, traditional English and the creative, highly context-dependent language...' (Họ ủng hộ mô hình chuyển đổi mã, dạy học sinh biết trân trọng sự phong phú của cả tiếng Anh trang trọng truyền thống lẫn ngôn ngữ mạng đầy sáng tạo...). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Concept matching.
questions.answerLocation: Section D: advocate for a model of code-switching, teaching students to appreciate the richness
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 49. the argument that forced brevity prompts users to distill complex arguments
questions.explanation: Đáp án B. Section B giải thích về ưu thế của sự ngắn gọn bắt buộc: 'proponents argue that this forced brevity encourages intellectual agility... prompting users to distill complex arguments into potent, high-impact statements.' (những người ủng hộ cho rằng sự ngắn gọn bắt buộc thúc đẩy sự nhạy bén trí tuệ... thúc đẩy người dùng chắt lọc các lập luận phức tạp thành những tuyên bố mạnh mẽ, có sức ảnh hưởng cao). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Detailed matching.
questions.answerLocation: Section B: forced brevity... prompting users to distill complex arguments into potent, high-impact statements.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 50. how local grammar is combined with global digital slang
questions.explanation: Đáp án E. Section E mô tả hiện tượng lai ngôn ngữ: 'hybrid internet dialects worldwide, where local grammar is combined with global digital slang.' (các phương ngôn internet lai trên toàn thế giới, nơi ngữ pháp địa phương được kết hợp với tiếng lóng số toàn cầu). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Specific detail matching.
questions.answerLocation: Section E: hybrid internet dialects... local grammar is combined with global digital slang.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 51. the use of specialized vernaculars to foster group identity
questions.explanation: Đáp án C. Section C phân tích vai trò xã hội của ngôn ngữ nhóm: 'digital sociolects that serve as powerful markers of group identity. Online subcultures... develop their own highly specialized vernaculars... fostering a deep sense of belonging...' (các phương ngữ số đóng vai trò như những dấu hiệu nhận diện nhóm mạnh mẽ... phát triển tiếng lóng chuyên biệt... nuôi dưỡng cảm giác thuộc về...). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Role of slang.
questions.answerLocation: Section C: digital sociolects that serve as powerful markers of group identity.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 52. the claim that language has historically evolved at a very slow pace
questions.explanation: Đáp án A. Section A mở đầu bằng việc đối chiếu lịch sử: 'Historically, language has evolved at a glacial pace, shaped by geographical isolation...' (Về mặt lịch sử, ngôn ngữ tiến hóa với tốc độ chậm như băng trôi, định hình bởi sự cô lập địa lý...). 'Glacial pace' = tốc độ cực kỳ chậm. Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Vocabulary matching.
questions.answerLocation: Section A: Historically, language has evolved at a glacial pace
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3

questions.instruction: 53. a concern over the intrusion of text-speak in academic work
questions.explanation: Đáp án D. Section D nêu sự lo ngại từ các nhà giáo dục: 'They voice deep concern... citing the frequent intrusion of text-speak, grammatical shortcuts, and erratic punctuation in academic work.' (Họ lên tiếng lo ngại sâu sắc... viện dẫn sự xâm nhập thường xuyên của ngôn ngữ nhắn tin, phím tắt ngữ pháp, và chấm câu thất thường trong bài làm học thuật). Chuyên đề: Reading Part 7 — Contextual matching.
questions.answerLocation: Section D: citing the frequent intrusion of text-speak... in academic work.
questions.source: Practice Test 3, Part 7, CPE Practice Tests 3
